data.body:
=== DISCUSSION NARRATIVE (chronological decision log) ===
Brainstorm to design an open->in_progress plan-review verification gate for rebar. One-question-at-a-time
protocol, grounded in subagent research + critique. Below is the decision arc, turn by turn.

[FRAMING] Joe: design a verification workflow that runs when a ticket goes open->in_progress, mirroring the
completion-verifier close gate but harder. Seed scenarios to catch: (1) work too big to one-shot -> decompose;
(2) too ambiguous -> clarify; (3) file references don't match the work; (4) plan ignores upstream/downstream
consumers; (5) plan ignores tests / TDD; (6) novel architecture not validated vs prior art (tuned: required for
complex/core changes, skipped for minor); (7) success-criteria/DoD don't match the description; (8) child tickets
don't fully/accurately cover the parent (evaluated when parent goes in_progress). Generalize across ticket type
and work size. Joe will add scenarios later.

[Q1 - what artifact is reviewed] I challenged: completion-verifier works because at close there's an
implementation to check; at open->in_progress nothing is built, so we judge a SPEC, and scenarios 4/5/6
presuppose a "plan" that may not exist. Joe: it depends on type -> a leaf (task/bug) must contain an
implementation plan (fail if absent); a story/epic may contain a plan OR have children that contain plans; if it
has children, evaluate whether children fully/accurately cover the parent; if neither plan nor children, fail
("add a plan or decompose"); a plan + no children must fail if too big to one-shot. THEN Joe refined: use
HAS_CHILDREN as the routing pivot, not ticket type (more reliable; type only colors prompt emphasis).

[BLOCKING ARCHITECTURE] I raised the hot-path tension (claim is the parallel-agent primitive; a heavy LLM+web
review can't block it inline) and offered A blocking-inline / B advisory / C decoupled-signed-attestation. Joe:
claiming should be a FAST succeed/fail; the process should EMPOWER the agent by surfacing flaws; the reviewer and
the plan-author must be SEPARATE agents (objectivity); also consider requiring empirical PROBES at plan time
(easier for an LLM to observe a real failure than mentally simulate) but only for significant/sensitive work.
I researched: hard-gate-is-anti-pattern does NOT transfer to autonomous agents (agents confabulate vs ask);
errors compound; separate critic >> self-critique (Kambhampati self-verification collapse). Landed on:
decoupled signed "ready" attestation; claim checks it fast.

[ATTESTATION + INVALIDATION] Joe: block on absence of a signed plan review, with an override+justification like
--force-close; invalidate the review if criteria/AC are edited (ticket is non-terminal). I proposed binding
scope; Joe chose scope (i): hash the ticket's own authored fields (description/AC/success-criteria/plan/
file_impact/child-set+criteria); drift detection is a future feature, out of scope. Joe asked whether we can bind
to a commit on the tickets branch -> yes, the git-native form of scope (i); refined to the latest MATERIAL event
commit (ignore comments/tags/links/assignee so progress comments don't force re-review).

[VERDICT/COMPOSITION] I mapped the completion-verifier pattern and proposed block-on-sound / advise-on-judgment.
Joe insisted: define WHAT we measure before WHAT blocks; rejected blocking on deterministic grounds ONLY;
AMBIGUOUS can be non-blocking; FAIL-block is per-dimension TBD (by severity/dimension/FP-likelihood); AMBIGUOUS as
an escalation signal to a higher-tier adjudicator is good; for each dimension consider full-agent-with-tools vs
one-turn eval.

[DSO DEEP-DIVE SAGA] Joe pointed me at the DSO project's brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan skills +
prompts + agents to mine hard-won review dimensions ("preserve the lessons without inheriting the rigidity").
First a single big extraction agent -> Joe: it OMITS key dimensions (fidelity reviews, gap analysis,
security/accessibility, red-team, understanding gate). I chunked into 5 agents -> still incomplete. Joe pushed:
diagnose the FLAW in my exploration and re-explore FROM SCRATCH; if a fresh pass misses what we know, the
methodology is broken. I diagnosed two flaws (preselected-location chunking blind to surfaces I didn't name;
lossy consolidation) and built a coverage-ledger methodology: full file inventory (42 skills/53 agents/81 hooks/
160 docs) -> signal grep -> 8 bounded lossless agents, each reconciling against the prior catalog. Result: every
prior dimension CONFIRMED + ~150 new + corrections; surfaced entire missed surfaces (the review HARNESS:
reviewer-base/delta/contracts/tristate-lattice; the gate/hook ENFORCEMENT layer; the Understanding Gate;
cross-epic scan; post-scrutiny handlers; the 13 untouched planning/quality skills).

[PRIOR ART + FAILURE MODES] Parallel research: GitHub LLM-plan-review ecosystem (Spec Kit /analyze 114k*, Kiro,
rfc-reviewer, anvil, flowglad, claude-code-plan-critique); LLM-planning failure-mode taxonomies (MAST ~44%
spec/plan failures; Plan-and-Solve 27% semantic-misunderstanding; TravelPlanner 0.6%; AbsenceBench - LLMs bad at
detecting omissions; Kambhampati 84% false-ACCEPT). Verdict-composition research said: (A) block-on-sound /
advise-on-judgment is the floor; (C) promote a judgment dim to block only once measured <~10% effective-FP;
reject (B) block-by-default-with-routine-override (static-analysis abandonment thresholds: >30% FP -> tool
ignored).

[OPUS CRITICAL REVIEW] An Opus adversarial review of v3 found the load-bearing error: we'd engineered the whole
anti-FP contract against false-POSITIVES while our own evidence says LLM plan-judges fail by false-ACCEPTANCE
(rubber-stamping). Also: cost incoherent (flat fan-out vs the cascade our own research prescribed); fail-open
silently guts the gate for non-mainstream-language clients + the attestation hides that; the 30+ dimension
registry is partly gold-plating with concrete double-flagging; calibrated abstention needs gold data that won't
exist; "agent-completable-in-session" is load-bearing yet unspecified; invalidation-on-edit risks a re-review
treadmill.

[THE REBALANCE - Joe sharpened it] Joe: the false-positive problem I over-indexed on is a CODE-REVIEW problem
(that's where I found the FP prompts in rebar) - NOT what we're designing. Also corrected: rebar is NOT heavily
bash (I'd conflated it with DSO; rebar is Python with legacy bash being strangled) - remediate that. And the
gate serves a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages/frameworks/structures); stop over-indexing on rebar's
own needs. Outcome: RECALL-FIRST rebalance - sound/deterministic checks block, LLM judgment advises/surfaces;
anti-FP stripped to minimal; FNR-on-seeded-bad-plans as primary metric. Dropped cite-or-omit-as-universal-gate
(fails for omissions -> ground the REQUIREMENT instead; AbsenceBench: never ask open-ended "what's missing")
and reachable-harm (no impl at plan time -> blockingness-to-criteria severity). Severity-minor-floor deferred
(code-review concept needing per-criterion tuning). Calibrated abstention dropped (no gold set -> surface-on-
uncertainty). Fail-open is DESIRED; the certificate means "process followed to the best of our ability, no red
flags" - NOT "perfect" - with COVERAGE recorded for transparency.

[CONFIGURABLE CHUNKING - Joe's reconciliation] Single holistic pass optimizes cost over quality; research shows
per-criterion decomposition improves quality (field avoids it only for cost). Joe: split the difference - let
the PROJECT configure CHUNKING (how many criteria per LLM call); local/small-model clients chunk finer (more
smaller calls, less context-limit risk), frontier-model clients chunk coarser (fewer calls, comparable quality);
"in the middle" default. Makes holistic-vs-decomposed a tunable that serves the diverse base.

[LAYER-2 LOOP - Joe's catch] Joe: when does the Layer-2 loop happen? Either the review is re-run from scratch
(then don't limit to prior findings if the plan changed) or it loops within one review (then why a second broad
pass?). Resolution: NO internal refine loop / NO second broad pass; the reviewer runs once per invocation and
returns findings; the only loop is the CLIENT re-invoking after revising. Convergence is FREE because Layer 1
(deterministic frozen binary rubric) is the SOLE blocker - all-MET is a guaranteed fixed point regardless of
advisory findings. On re-run, review the current plan FRESH; diff-only is an optional cost-saver, not a
convergence mechanism. (Convergence research confirmed: cap + severity-gating + diff-only + freeze-rubric; field
keeps LLM plan-review ADVISORY not blocking BECAUSE of the rubber-stamp problem + non-determinism.)

[BUDGET / ESCALATION / PROPORTIONATE / BUGS] Joe: scrutiny must scale with ticket size/complexity (don't give a
small task an epic's review). Budget caps: handle hitting them - criterion/chunk-ATOMIC checkpointing (no
half-criterion waste); on hit -> a NON-TERMINAL "incomplete" outcome (NOT failed - cap is external to the ticket;
NOT certified - process didn't complete); client raises budget / re-runs / escalates. Escalation factors = SIZE
+ COMPLEXITY + CENTRALITY (DSO has hooks/lib/centrality.sh + blast-radius-score.py). Risk posture is
PROJECT-CONFIGURABLE with a sensible default; Joe wants to APPROVE the time/cost/risk tradeoffs. Bugs: park them
- ship with bug-type SKIPPED + a follow-on epic (their format/content differs; bugs often lack a plan at claim
because you investigate first); revisit after tuning; interim has_plan-driven (planless ticket = N/A, not FAIL).

[EXTENSIBILITY] Joe: design to GROW - tuning/adding/removing a dimension must be trivial+reliable. -> a dimension
is a declarative REGISTRY DESCRIPTOR; generic orchestrator; reuse rebar.llm's catalog.json+contracts+prompt+
runner extension points. Project-specific review criteria = a FUTURE SEAM (design-for, not built this epic);
project-invariant compliance is the first such. Implement ALL criteria in ONE WAVE, decomposed into
appropriately-sized tasks with dependencies.

[POLYGLOT TOOLING] Research: semantic resolution (P2/G1) is only honest via LSP-class tools; the happy finding is
solidlsp (Serena's LSP lib) is ALREADY in this repo. Stack: solidlsp + ast-grep (P2/G1/G2), syft + per-ecosystem
registry + LLM-fallback (P3), scc + lizard (P4). FAIL-OPEN is an ironclad invariant (unsupported lang/no server/
crash/timeout -> skipped=PASS, never FAIL; hard "missing" only from top-tier servers). Avoid stack-graphs
(archived) / Glean (over-engineered); osv-scanner != existence oracle.

[DSO TICKET MINING - tuning] Joe: mine DSO's ticket data (rebar-like store) for a temporary example library to
tune complexity-proportionate scrutiny + over/under-review risk. Findings: store is an event-sourced rebar store
(orphan tickets branch + .tickets-tracker/, one dir per ticket, .cache.json head state); ~8,317 native tickets
(task 4455/bug 2112/story 1362/epic 388; ~82% closed). KEY: file_impact is effectively UNUSED (1 ticket);
AC checklists exist mostly on tasks (50%); only 59 tickets carry an in-comment COMPLEXITY label (weak-gold seed,
bug-skewed); signatures/verify_commands sparse. Proposed depth tiers grounded in the distribution (tasks/bugs by
files/layers: <=1 minimal / 2-3 standard / >3 deep; stories/epics by children: 0-1 / 2-6 / >=7); escalation =
size x complexity x centrality (persist blast-radius + centrality scores at plan time - the single highest-
leverage tuning fix); default chunking 3-5 ACs per call. Rework markers correlate with complexity (epics with
>=7 children carry rework markers 42% vs 12% for <7 - validates children>=7 as a deep-review trigger). Honest
limits: labels sparse + bug-skewed, outcomes text-inferred, DSO-specific topology won't transfer cleanly - good
as a TUNING + regression set, not a clean held-out eval set.

[CONVERGED DESIGN - two layers] LAYER 1 deterministic + structural frozen-binary-rubric gate = the ONLY blocker
(convergent by construction); LAYER 2 advisory LLM review (structured criteria, chunking-configurable) + ONE
bounded broad open-ended pass for unknown-unknowns, never blocks, surfaces findings; client revises + re-runs.
Reviewer != the model that authored the plan (self-preference). Cert = process-followed + no-red-flags + coverage;
material-edit invalidation. See the appended design docs (our-review-dimensions-v3.md is the latest; v4
design-of-record pending, to fold in the DSO mining tuning + budget-incomplete status + escalation).

[CORRECTIONS I LOGGED] (1) "rebar is heavily bash" was wrong (conflation with DSO; rebar is Python). (2)
Optimized the wrong loss (FP vs FN) until Joe + Opus corrected it. (3) Over-consolidated DSO extraction twice
before the coverage-ledger re-do. (4) cite-or-omit + reachable-harm were code-review imports, dropped.

[OPEN / NEXT] Write the v4 design-of-record (Layer-1/Layer-2 + configurable chunking + budget-incomplete +
escalation by size/complexity/centrality + DSO-grounded depth tiers). Then decompose into the rebar
implementation epic (one wave, tasks with deps). PENDING JOE: approve the proportionate-depth posture/thresholds,
the budget posture, and confirm bug-skip + follow-on epic.
